bullets: Research shows that 20% of Canadians have high Lipoprotein(a) levels, increasing their heart attack and stroke risk.

Advocates are pushing for awareness and screening initiatives to help identify at-risk individuals and improve heart health outcomes.

This case underscores ongoing concerns about street racing and public safety, prompting calls for stricter enforcement against reckless driving.

stories.title: Aussie stuns Brazil's fab four for first World Surf League title
bullets: Australian surfer George Pittar won his first World Surf League title at the Margaret River Pro, defeating four Brazilian champions.
Pittar's victory marks a significant achievement in his career, showcasing his talent against top competitors in the sport.
This win could elevate his profile in the surfing community, potentially leading to sponsorship opportunities and future successes.

stories.title: Fish oil may be hurting your brain, new study finds
bullets: New research indicates fish oil, particularly EPA, may harm brain recovery in individuals with repeated mild head injuries.
The study suggests EPA weakens blood vessel stability and disrupts healing signals, potentially worsening cognitive decline.
This finding challenges long-held beliefs about fish oil's benefits, prompting reevaluation of dietary recommendations for brain health.

stories.title: Trump security threat live updates: President evacuated from White House dinner after shots fired
bullets: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 26, 2026, after shots were fired nearby.
Security footage showed a suspect rushing a checkpoint; no injuries were reported, and the suspect was apprehended.
This incident raises concerns about security at high-profile events and the safety of public figures.

stories.title: The real skills crisis is executive imagination
bullets: Leaders are facing a skills crisis due to a lack of imagination in adapting to AI and market changes.
The article emphasizes the need for executives to reinvent and retire outdated assumptions to remain competitive.
Addressing this crisis is crucial for workforce readiness and organizational resilience in an evolving technological landscape.

stories.title: LIVE:  ABC News Live
bullets: ABC News provides continuous coverage of breaking news and live events, ensuring real-time updates for viewers.
The platform aims to keep the public informed about significant developments as they unfold throughout the day.

stories.title: Trump holds press briefing after shooting outside White House Correspondents' Dinner
bullets: President Trump held a press briefing after a shooter was apprehended outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner.
FBI Director Kash Patel and Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche joined Trump, discussing security measures post-incident.
The shooting raised concerns about safety at public events, prompting a review of security protocols for future gatherings.

stories.title: As Walk for Peace begins in Sri Lanka, activists call for animal rights
bullets: A group of 24 Buddhist monks began the Walk for Peace in Sri Lanka, promoting compassion and nonviolence.
The monks are accompanied by Aloka, a stray dog, symbolizing their message of peace and animal rights.
This event aims to raise awareness about animal welfare and foster a culture of kindness in Sri Lankan society.

stories.title: Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect allegedly wrote about targeting U.S. officials, says Trump
bullets: Trump revealed that the suspect in the Correspondents' Dinner shooting had previously written about targeting U.S. officials.
This alarming detail raises questions about the motivations behind the attack and potential security threats.
The incident underscores the need for enhanced vigilance and preventive measures against targeted violence in public spaces.

stories.title: BYD's next all-electric hypercar is a convertible that's coming to Europe first
bullets: BYD unveiled the Denza Z, an all-electric hypercar, at the Beijing Auto Show on April 25, 2026.
The hypercar boasts over 1,000 horsepower and can accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in under two seconds.
This launch signifies BYD's expansion into the luxury electric vehicle market, competing with established brands.

stories.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman apologizes for not reporting ChatGPT account of Tumbler Ridge suspect to police
bullets: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, apologized on April 25, 2026, for not reporting alarming ChatGPT conversations linked to Tumbler Ridge shooter Jesse Van Rootselaar.
The account was banned in June for policy violations, but Altman acknowledged the failure to alert law enforcement about potential violence.
This incident raises questions about AI accountability and the responsibilities of tech companies in preventing real-world harm.

stories.title: This Buzzy Pocket-Sized E-Reader Is Under $60 on Amazon for the Next Few Hours
bullets: A pocket-sized e-reader is available on Amazon for under $60, attracting attention for its affordability.
Despite its small size and some design flaws, the e-reader offers significant value for budget-conscious consumers.
This limited-time offer encourages readers to explore digital reading options without a hefty investment.

stories.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
bullets: Coordinated attacks by militants and separatists occurred across Mali, including strikes on Bamako's international airport.
The al-Qaida-linked group JNIM claimed responsibility, collaborating with the Azawad Liberation Front in these assaults.
This marks one of the largest coordinated attacks in Mali in recent years, escalating concerns over national security.

stories.title: Google Wallet adds support for three more digital passport IDs on Android
bullets: Google Wallet now supports digital passport IDs from Canada, Australia, and the UK as of April 25, 2026.
This update enhances user convenience, allowing seamless travel with digital IDs, which could reduce physical document reliance.
The expansion aims to increase Google Wallet's adoption in international travel, potentially impacting competitors in the digital ID space.

stories.title: Trump Cancels Iran Peace Talks at Last Minute: 'We Have All the Cards'
bullets: Donald Trump canceled Iran peace talks scheduled for April 25, 2026, stating, 'We have all the cards' in a strategic maneuver.
This marks the second cancellation of a high-level U.S. mission to Pakistan within a week, indicating shifting diplomatic priorities.
The cancellation could impact U.S.-Iran relations, complicating efforts for peace in the region amid ongoing tensions.
